title: psoralea picta
intro: Psoralea picta is a plant species known as "Indian turnip" or "white puararia," which is native to Asia and used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in coumarins, specifically psoralen, which is known for its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote cell regeneration. The health benefits of Psoralea picta include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis, as well as its ability to improve wound healing and reduce inflammation. In traditional medicine, Psoralea picta is prepared as a decoction or infused in tea, which is often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
